summary: RQ6: ADAPT XCE static 'ALWAYS call X FIRST' pattern to dynamic advisor brief. Render-layer change only (~30 LOC): add FIRST_ACTION_HINT map (17 lines) + modify two capText calls in render.ts lines 149-152, 155-158 from 'use' to 'MUST invoke X FIRST — hint'. Cannot ADOPT unconditional static pattern (our advisor is dynamic, confidence-gated). Intent→first-action table covers all 16 skills. mcp-coco-index and system-spec-kit most closely mirror XCE's 'use before file reading' pattern. Token measurement hook surfaced for RQ8.

label: XCE static steering mandate phrasing extracted from 6 files: (1) Unconditional ALWAYS — all 6 files: 'Always use xanther-xce MCP tools before reading files' (CLAUDE.md:5, kiro.md:4, opencode-prompt.txt:1, .clinerules:1, .cursorrules:1, .windsurfrules:1). (2) Ordering imperative FIRST — kiro.md:6 capitalizes 'FIRST step'; 5 others say 'first on any task' or 'first action'. (3) PREFER over file reading — kiro.md:12 'Prefer XCE context over grep, find, or reading files'; CLAUDE.md:12 'faster and more accurate than reading files individually'

verdict: EVIDENCE — 3-element mandate pattern (ALWAYS + FIRST + PREFER over file reading) is structurally identical across all 6 steering files

label: Current render.ts brief uses 'use' (suggestion) vs XCE's 'Always use' (mandate). Gap: no ordering directive (FIRST), no replacement framing (PREFER over file reading), mandate strength is 'use' not 'MUST invoke'. Normal brief at render.ts:155-158: 'Advisor: ${freshness}; use ${topLabel} ${score}/${uncertainty} pass.' Ambiguous brief at render.ts:149-152: 'Advisor: ${freshness}; ambiguous: ${top} ${c}/${u} vs ${second} ${c}/${u} pass.' Threshold gate at render.ts:124-133 filters to confidence ≥ 0.8 and uncertainty ≤ 0.35 — high-confidence recommendations deserve stronger mandate than 'use'.

evidence.quote: return capText(`Advisor: ${result.freshness}; use ${topLabel} ${formatScore(top.confidence)}/${formatScore(top.uncertainty)} pass.`, Math.min(tokenCap, DEFAULT_TOKEN_CAP));

return capText(`Advisor: ${result.freshness}; ambiguous: ${topLabel} ${formatScore(top.confidence)}/${formatScore(top.uncertainty)} vs ${secondLabel} ${formatScore(second.confidence)}/${formatScore(second.uncertainty)} pass.`, Math.min(tokenCap, AMBIGUOUS_TOKEN_CAP));
renderSharedBrief() constructs AdvisorBriefRenderableResult and delegates to renderAdvisorBrief() — sole prompt-boundary surface

verdict: ADAPT — 'use' → 'MUST invoke... FIRST' with per-skill action hint

label: Intent → first-action map for 16 skills: (1) cli-claude-code → dispatch Claude Code CLI, (2) cli-codex → dispatch Codex CLI, (3) cli-gemini → dispatch Gemini CLI, (4) cli-opencode → dispatch OpenCode CLI, (5) deep-agent-improvement → evaluate with 5-dim scoring, (6) deep-research → run /deep:start-research-loop:auto, (7) deep-review → run /deep:start-review-loop:auto, (8) mcp-chrome-devtools → route to bdg CLI or Code Mode, (9) mcp-coco-index → semantic search BEFORE grep, (10) mcp-code-mode → execute via call_tool_chain, (11) sk-code → detect surface then verify, (12) sk-code-review → severity-first security review, (13) sk-doc → classify then load template, (14) sk-git → worktree or commit workflow, (15) sk-prompt → select CLEAR framework, (16) system-spec-kit → validate gates BEFORE reading/editing files. Skills 9 and 16 closest to XCE's 'before file reading' pattern. Remaining 14 skills have CLI/workflow first-actions (sequencing directives, not file-reading replacements).

evidence.quote: Full 16 skill descriptions from system prompt with intent framing
Current brief only references skill slug — no intent or first-action hint
Prefer XCE context over grep, find, or reading files for understanding code structure

verdict: MAP — first-action hints differentiate sequencing skills (CLI dispatch) from context-first skills (coco-index/system-spec-kit 'BEFORE reading files')

label: Proposed render-layer change: (a) Add FIRST_ACTION_HINT constant map (17 lines, after render.ts:46). (b) Normal brief (render.ts:155-158): 'use ${topLabel}' → 'MUST invoke ${topLabel} FIRST (${score}) — ${hint}'. (c) Ambiguous brief (render.ts:149-152): '...vs ... pass.' → '...vs ... — MUST invoke one FIRST.' Estimated ~30 LOC. Brief grows from ~15-20 tokens to ~25-45 tokens — fits within DEFAULT_TOKEN_CAP=80 tokens (320 chars). Token growth per session: <10 tokens (~0.02% of 60k session).

evidence.quote: Two capText calls (lines 149-152 ambiguous + lines 155-158 normal) are the sole output surfaces
DEFAULT_TOKEN_CAP = 80; AMBIGUOUS_TOKEN_CAP = 120; MAX_TOKEN_CAP = 120 — new format fits comfortably in current caps
~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively — attributed to steering obedience, not steering text size

verdict: ADAPT — ~30 LOC, render-layer only, no scorer surgery, zero blast radius

label: Verdict ADAPT (not ADOPT): Cannot ADOPT XCE's unconditional static 'ALWAYS' because (1) our advisor is dynamic (fires only at confidence ≥ 0.8), (2) our advisor selects among 16 skills (XCE hard-codes one tool). CAN ADAPT by (a) strengthening 'use' to 'MUST invoke' — justified because brief only renders when confidence is high, (b) adding 'FIRST' ordering imperative, (c) adding per-skill action hints that mirror 'before reading files' framing for context-first skills. Not DEFER (change is cheap, ~30 LOC). Not SKIP (stronger directive is prerequisite for RQ8 token-reduction measurement).

evidence.quote: Confidence ≥ 0.8 gate means brief only renders when scorer is confident — 'MUST invoke' is justified, not hyperbolic
Always use xanther-xce MCP tools before reading files — static, unconditional
Call xce_get_context as your FIRST step when starting any task

verdict: ADAPT — render-layer mandate-strengthening only, dynamic conditional equivalent of XCE's static unconditional pattern
